CONTINUED TO AUGUST 22, 2024, TO BE HEARD ON THE COURT'S DISCOVERY CALENDAR IN ROOM 525. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/CEK)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AUG-22-2024 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT JOANN EDSON'S MOTION TO DEEM FACTS AS ADMITTED; AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES OF $300.00. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, AND IN VIEW OF DEFENDANT'S WITHDRAWAL OF HER MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING REQUESTS ADMITTED, SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $600 AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND ITS COUNSEL OF RECORD, JOINTLY, PAYABLE WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE FILING DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THE FOREGOING AND SUBMIT IT TO PSW@HASSARD.COM FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (525/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-05-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-08-30 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Facts As Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Supporting Declaration; And Request For Attorney Fees Of $300.00 (Crc 3.1702, Ccp 2033.010 And 2033.280(B)
Off Calendar Aug-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ALEXANDER BALZER CARR, Esq. of Hunt & Henriques, LLP (Tel: 800-680-2426) for plaintiff Citibank, N.A.;
Atty. ROBERT SCHOEN, Esq. of Legal Assistance to the Elderly (Tel: 415-538-3333) for defendant JoAnn Edson.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOANN EDSON's Motion To Deem Facts As Admitted; And Request For Attorney Fees Of $300.00.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. Counsel for plaintiff authorizes the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on his behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant served written discovery on plaintiff that included a Request for Admissions. Plaintiff did not timely respond to the admissions request, as a result of which defendant filed a motion for an order deeming the admission requests admitted together with a request for sanctions in the amount of $300. After the defendant's motion was filed, but before the noticed hearing date, plaintiff served responses to the admission requests. Defendant has now informed the Court that, in view of plaintiff's late-submitted responses, she is withdrawing her motion. Defendant, however, requests a sanctions award in the amount of $600, representing the labor and cost incurred in preparing the motion in the first place and then having to withdraw the motion in compliance with governing provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure.

For good cause shown, and in view of defendant's withdrawal of her motion for an order deeming requests admitted, sanctions are awarded to defendant in the amount of $600 against plaintiff and its counsel of record, jointly, payable within 10 days of the filing date of this order.

Counsel for defendant shall prepare a proposed order consistent with the foregoing and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)
